description_long: This object is a preserved crab with a robust, spiny carapace and long, jointed legs radiating outward from its body. The carapace is textured with bumps and ridges, giving it a rugged appearance, and is a muted beige to light brown color. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae) at the front, which are slightly larger and more robust than the other legs. Its legs are slender, segmented, and curve outward, with pointed tips. The underside reveals a lighter, smoother surface with visible segmentation and appendages. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and short antennae are also visible.
description: A spiny, beige crab with a rugged carapace, long jointed legs, and prominent front claws. The surface is rough and textured, with visible eyes on short stalks and short antennae.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's spiny carapace, both claws, and the full spread of its legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crab's body depth, leg curvature, and the segmentation of the underside.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the crab's profile, leg articulation, and the shape of the claws.
